v.CNV: 礼拜日大清早的时候，妇女们带着预备好的香料，来到坟墓那里，
v.ESV: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they went to the tomb, taking the spices they had prepared.
v.GAE: 안식 후 첫날 새벽에 이 여자들이 그 준비한 향품을 가지고 무덤에 가서
v.KJV: Now upon the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain others with them.
v.NASB: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they came to the tomb bringing the spices which they had prepared.
v.NIV: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb.
v.NRSV: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they came to the tomb, taking the spices that they had prepared.
v.SAENEW: 이레의 첫날 이른 새벽에, 여자들은 준비한 향료를 가지고 무덤으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 그 주의 첫날 이른 새벽에 여인들은 준비한 향품을 가지고 무덤으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 发现石头已经从坟墓辊开了，
v.ESV: And they found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
v.GAE: 돌이 무덤에서 굴려 옮겨진 것을 보고
v.KJV: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
v.NASB: And they found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
v.NIV: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
v.NRSV: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
v.SAENEW: 그들은 무덤 어귀를 막은 돌이 무덤에서 굴려져 나간 것을 보았다.
v.WLB: 그런데 무덤 입구를 막은 돌덩이가 굴려져 있는 것을 발견했습니다.

v.CNV: 就进去，却找不着主耶稣的身体。
v.ESV: but when they went in they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
v.GAE: 들어가니 주 예수의 시체가 보이지 아니하더라
v.KJV: And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
v.NASB: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
v.NIV: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
v.NRSV: but when they went in, they did not find the body.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 안으로 들어가 보니, 주 예수의 시신이 없었다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그들이 안으로 들어가 보니 주 예수의 시신이 없었습니다.

v.CNV: 她们正为此事猜疑的时候，忽然有两个人，穿着闪烁耀目的衣服，站在她们旁边。
v.ESV: While they were perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling apparel.
v.GAE: 이로 인하여 근심할 때에 문득 찬란한 옷을 입은 두 사람이 곁에 섰는지라
v.KJV: And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
v.NASB: While they were perplexed about this, behold, two men suddenly stood near them in dazzling clothing;
v.NIV: While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them.
v.NRSV: While they were perplexed about this, suddenly two men in dazzling clothes stood beside them.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그들이 이 일을 어떻게 해야 할지를 몰라서 당황하고 있는데, 눈부신 옷을 입은 두 남자가 갑자기 그들 앞에 나섰다.
v.WLB: 그들이 이 일에 대해 어찌해야 할지 몰라 당황하고 있는데 빛나는 옷을 입은 두 사람이 갑자기 그들 곁에 섰습니다.

v.CNV: 她们害怕，把脸伏在地上。那两个人对她们说：“为什么在死人中找活人呢？
v.ESV: And as they were frightened and bowed their faces to the ground, the men said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?
v.GAE: 여자들이 두려워 얼굴을 땅에 대니 두 사람이 이르되 어찌하여 살아 있는 자를 죽은 자 가운데서 찾느냐
v.KJV: And as they were afraid, and bowed down their faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
v.NASB: and as the women were terrified and bowed their faces to the ground, the men said to them, 'Why do you seek the living One among the dead?
v.NIV: In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead?
v.NRSV: The women were terrified and bowed their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here, but has risen.
v.SAENEW: 여자들은 두려워서 얼굴을 아래로 숙이고 있는데, 그 남자들이 그들에게 말하였다. "어찌하여 너희들은 살아 계신 분을 죽은 사람들 가운데서 찾고 있느냐?
v.WLB: 여인들은 너무 무서워 얼굴을 땅에 대고 엎드렸습니다. 그러자 그 사람들이 말했습니다. “살아 계신 분을 왜 죽은 사람들 사이에서 찾고 있느냐?

v.CNV: 他不在这里，已经复活了。你们应当记得他还在加利利的时候，怎样告诉你们，
v.ESV: He is not here, but has risen. Remember how he told you, while he was still in Galilee,
v.GAE: 여기 계시지 않고 살아나셨느니라 갈릴리에 계실 때에 너희에게 어떻게 말씀하셨는지를 기억하라
v.KJV: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
v.NASB: 'He is not here, but He has risen. Remember how He spoke to you while He was still in Galilee,
v.NIV: He is not here; he has risen! Remember how he told you, while he was still with you in Galilee:
v.NRSV: Remember how he told you, while he was still in Galilee,
v.SAENEW: 그분은 여기에 계시지 않고, 살아나셨다. 갈릴리에 계실 때에, 너희들에게 하신 말씀을 기억해 보아라.
v.WLB: 그분은 여기 계시지 않고 살아나셨다! 예수께서 갈릴리에서 너희와 함께 계실 때 하신 말씀을 기억해 보라.

v.CNV: 说：‘人子必须被交在罪人手里，钉在十字架上，第三日复活。’”
v.ESV: that the Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men and be crucified and on the third day rise.”
v.GAE: 이르시기를 인자가 죄인의 손에 넘겨져 십자가에 못 박히고 제삼일에 다시 살아나야 하리라 하셨느니라 한대
v.KJV: Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
v.NASB: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.'
v.NIV: ‘The Son of Man must be delivered over to the hands of sinners, be crucified and on the third day be raised again.’ ”
v.NRSV: that the Son of Man must be handed over to sinners, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again.”
v.SAENEW: '인자는 반드시 죄인의 손에 넘어가서, 십자가에 처형되고, 사흘째 되는 날에 살아나야 한다'고 하셨다."
v.WLB: ‘인자가 마땅히 죄인의 손에 넘겨져 십자가에 못 박히고 3일째 되는 날에 다시 살아나야 한다’고 하시지 않았느냐?”

v.CNV: 她们就想起他的话，
v.ESV: And they remembered his words,
v.GAE: 그들이 예수의 말씀을 기억하고
v.KJV: And they remembered his words,
v.NASB: And they remembered His words,
v.NIV: Then they remembered his words.
v.NRSV: Then they remembered his words,
v.SAENEW: 여자들은 예수의 말씀을 회상하였다.
v.WLB: 여인들은 예수의 말씀을 기억했습니다.

v.CNV: 于是从坟地回去，把这一切事告诉十一个使徒和其余的人。
v.ESV: and returning from the tomb they told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
v.GAE: 무덤에서 돌아가 이 모든 것을 열한 사도와 다른 모든 이에게 알리니
v.KJV: And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
v.NASB: and returned from the tomb and reported all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
v.NIV: When they came back from the tomb, they told all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.
v.NRSV: and returning from the tomb, they told all this to the eleven and to all the rest.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 무덤에서 돌아와서, 열한 제자와 그 밖의 모든 사람에게 이 모든 일을 알렸다.
v.WLB: 여인들은 무덤에서 돌아와 열한 제자들과 다른 모든 사람들에게 이 사실을 모두 알렸습니다.

v.CNV: 那些妇女就是抹大拉的马利亚，约亚拿和雅各的母亲马利亚，以及其他在一起的妇女，她们把这些事向使徒说了。
v.ESV: Now it was Mary Magdalene and Joanna and Mary the mother of James and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles,
v.GAE: (이 여자들은 막달라 마리아와 요안나와 야고보의 모친 마리아라 또 그들과 함께 한 다른 여자들도 이것을 사도들에게 알리니라)
v.KJV: It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and other women that were with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
v.NASB: Now they were Mary Magdalene and Joanna and Mary the mother of James; also the other women with them were telling these things to the apostles.
v.NIV: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the others with them who told this to the apostles.
v.NRSV: Now it was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told this to the apostles.
v.SAENEW: 이 여자들은 막달라 마리아와 요안나와 야고보의 어머니인 마리아이다. 이 여자들과 함께 있던 다른 여자들도, 이 일을 사도들에게 말하였다.
v.WLB: (그들은 막달라 마리아, 요안나, 야고보의 어머니 마리아였습니다. 그들과 함께 있었던 다른 몇몇 여인들도 이 일을 사도들에게 말했습니다.)

v.CNV: 使徒以为这些话是无稽之谈，就不相信。
v.ESV: but these words seemed to them an idle tale, and they did not believe them.
v.GAE: 사도들은 그들의 말이 허탄한 듯이 들려 믿지 아니하나
v.KJV: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
v.NASB: But these words appeared to them as nonsense, and they would not believe them.
v.NIV: But they did not believe the women, because their words seemed to them like nonsense.
v.NRSV: But these words seemed to them an idle tale, and they did not believe them.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 사도들에게는 이 말이 어처구니없는 말로 들렸으므로, 그들은 여자들의 말을 믿지 않았다.
v.WLB: 그러나 사도들은 여인들의 말이 어처구니없게 들렸으므로 그 말을 믿지 않았습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得却起来跑到坟墓那里，屈身往里观看，只见细麻布在那里，就回去了，对所发生的事非常惊奇。
v.ESV: But Peter rose and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths by themselves; and he went home marveling at what had happened.
v.GAE: 베드로는 일어나 무덤에 달려가서 구부려 들여다 보니 세마포만 보이는지라 그 된 일을 놀랍게 여기며 집으로 돌아가니라
v.KJV: Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
v.NASB: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen wrappings only; and he went away to his home, marveling at what had happened.
v.NIV: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. Bending over, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.
v.NRSV: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths by themselves; then he went home, amazed at what had happened.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 베드로는 일어나서 무덤으로 달려가, 몸을 굽혀서 들여다보았다. 거기에는 시신을 감았던 삼베만 놓여 있었다. 그는 일어난 일을 이상히 여기면서 집으로 돌아갔다.
v.WLB: 하지만 베드로는 일어나 무덤으로 달려갔습니다. 몸을 굽혀 안을 들여다보니 고운 삼베 천만 놓여 있었습니다. 그는 이상하게 생각하며 돌아갔습니다.

v.CNV: 同一天，门徒中有两个人往一个叫以马午斯的村子去，那村子距耶路撒冷约十一公里；
v.ESV: That very day two of them were going to a village named Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem,
v.GAE: 그 날에 그들 중 둘이 예루살렘에서 이십오 리 되는 엠마오라 하는 마을로 가면서
v.KJV: And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem about threescore furlongs.
v.NASB: And behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was about seven miles from Jerusalem.
v.NIV: Now that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
v.NRSV: Now on that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem,
v.SAENEW: 마침 그 날에 그들 가운데 두 사람이 예루살렘에서 한 삼십 리 떨어져 있는 엠마오라는 마을로 가고 있었다.
v.WLB: 바로 그날 그들 중 두 사람이 예루살렘에서 약 60스타디온 남짓 떨어져 있는 엠마오라는 마을로 가는 중이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们彼此谈论所发生的这一切事。
v.ESV: and they were talking with each other about all these things that had happened.
v.GAE: 이 모든 된 일을 서로 이야기하더라
v.KJV: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
v.NASB: And they were talking with each other about all these things which had taken place.
v.NIV: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.
v.NRSV: and talking with each other about all these things that had happened.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 일어난 이 모든 일을 서로 이야기하고 있었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 일어난 이 모든 일에 대해 서로 이야기하고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 正在交谈议论的时候，耶稣亲自走近他们，与他们同行，
v.ESV: While they were talking and discussing together, Jesus himself drew near and went with them.
v.GAE: 그들이 서로 이야기하며 문의할 때에 예수께서 가까이 이르러 그들과 동행하시나
v.KJV: And it came to pass, that, while they communed together and reasoned, Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
v.NASB: While they were talking and discussing, Jesus Himself approached and began traveling with them.
v.NIV: As they talked and discussed these things with each other, Jesus himself came up and walked along with them;
v.NRSV: While they were talking and discussing, Jesus himself came near and went with them,
v.SAENEW: 그들이 이야기하며 토론하고 있는데, 예수께서 가까이 가서, 그들과 함께 걸으셨다.
v.WLB: 그들이 이야기하며 토론하고 있는데 예수께서 가까이 가서 그들과 함께 걸어가셨습니다.

v.CNV: 但他们的眼睛却给蒙蔽了，认不出他来。
v.ESV: But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.
v.GAE: 그들의 눈이 가리어져서 그인 줄 알아보지 못하거늘
v.KJV: But their eyes were holden that they should not know him.
v.NASB: But their eyes were prevented from recognizing Him.
v.NIV: but they were kept from recognizing him.
v.NRSV: but their eyes were kept from recognizing him.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그들은 눈이 가려져서 예수를 알아보지 못하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 그들은 눈이 가려져서 예수를 알아보지 못했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“你们走路的时候，彼此讨论的是什么事呢？”他们就站住，面带愁容；
v.ESV: And he said to them, “What is this conversation that you are holding with each other as you walk?” And they stood still, looking sad.
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 너희가 길 가면서 서로 주고받고 하는 이야기가 무엇이냐 하시니 두 사람이 슬픈 빛을 띠고 머물러 서더라
v.KJV: And he said unto them, What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'What are these words that you are exchanging with one another as you are walking?' And they stood still, looking sad.
v.NIV: He asked them, “What are you discussing together as you walk along?” They stood still, their faces downcast.
v.NRSV: And he said to them, “What are you discussing with each other while you walk along?” They stood still, looking sad.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨다. "당신들이 걸으면서 서로 주고 받는 이 말들은 무슨 이야기입니까?" 그들은 침통한 표정을 지으며 걸음을 멈추었다.
v.WLB: 그분께서 그들에게 물으셨습니다. “당신들이 걸어가면서 서로 주고받는 이 말이 무슨 이야기요?” 그들은 슬픈 기색으로 가던 길을 멈추어 섰습니다.

v.CNV: 一个名叫革流巴的回答他：“在耶路撒冷作客的，难道只有你不知道这几天在那里所发生的事吗？”
v.ESV: Then one of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in these days?”
v.GAE: 그 한 사람인 글로바라 하는 자가 대답하여 이르되 당신이 예루살렘에 체류하면서도 요즘 거기서 된 일을 혼자만 알지 못하느냐
v.KJV: And the one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days?
v.NASB: One of them, named Cleopas, answered and said to Him, 'Are You the only one visiting Jerusalem and unaware of the things which have happened here in these days?'
v.NIV: One of them, named Cleopas, asked him, “Are you the only one visiting Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in these days?”
v.NRSV: Then one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who does not know the things that have taken place there in these days?”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 그들 가운데 하나인 글로바라는 사람이 예수께 말하였다. "예루살렘에 머물러 있었으면서, 이 며칠 동안에 거기에서 일어난 일을 당신 혼자만 모른단 말입니까?"
v.WLB: 그중 글로바라는 사람이 그분께 물었습니다. “예루살렘에 있으면서 최근 일어난 일을 혼자만 모르신단 말씀입니까?”

v.CNV: 他说：“什么事呢？”他们说：“就是拿撒勒人耶稣的事。他是先知，在　神和众人面前，说话行事都有大能。
v.ESV: And he said to them, “What things?” And they said to him, “Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, a man who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,
v.GAE: 이르시되 무슨 일이냐 이르되 나사렛 예수의 일이니 그는 하나님과 모든 백성 앞에서 말과 일에 능하신 선지자이거늘
v.KJV: And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'What things?' And they said to Him, 'The things about Jesus the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word in the sight of God and all the people,
v.NIV: “What things?” he asked. “About Jesus of Nazareth,” they replied. “He was a prophet, powerful in word and deed before God and all the people.
v.NRSV: He asked them, “What things?” They replied, “The things about Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨다. "무슨 일입니까?" 그들이 그에게 말하였다. "나사렛 예수에 관한 일입니다. 그는 하나님과 모든 백성 앞에서, 행동과 말씀에 힘이 있는 예언자였습니다.
v.WLB: 그분이 물으셨습니다. “무슨 일이오?” 그들이 대답했습니다. “나사렛 예수에 관한 일 말입니다. 그분은 하나님과 모든 백성들 앞에서 행동과 말씀에 능력이 있는 예언자셨습니다.

v.CNV: 我们的祭司长和官长，竟把他交出去，判了死罪，钉在十字架上。
v.ESV: and how our chief priests and rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.
v.GAE: 우리 대제사장들과 관리들이 사형 판결에 넘겨 주어 십자가에 못 박았느니라
v.KJV: And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
v.NASB: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered Him to the sentence of death, and crucified Him.
v.NIV: The chief priests and our rulers handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him;
v.NRSV: and how our chief priests and leaders handed him over to be condemned to death and crucified him.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 우리의 대제사장들과 지도자들이 그를 넘겨주어서, 사형선고를 받게 하고, 십자가에 못박아 죽였습니다.
v.WLB: 그런데 우리 대제사장들과 지도자들이 그분을 넘겨주어 사형 선고를 받게 했고 십자가에 못 박았습니다.

v.CNV: 但我们素来盼望要救赎以色列的就是他。不但这样，这些事发生到今天，已经是第三天了。
v.ESV: But we had hoped that he was the one to redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.
v.GAE: 우리는 이 사람이 이스라엘을 속량할 자라고 바랐노라 이뿐 아니라 이 일이 일어난 지가 사흘째요
v.KJV: But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
v.NASB: 'But we were hoping that it was He who was going to redeem Israel. Indeed, besides all this, it is the third day since these things happened.
v.NIV: but we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel. And what is more, it is the third day since all this took place.
v.NRSV: But we had hoped that he was the one to redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things took place.
v.SAENEW: 우리는 그분이야말로 이스라엘을 구원하실 분이라는 것을 알고서, 그분에게 소망을 걸고 있었던 것입니다. 그뿐만 아니라, 그런 일이 있은 지 벌써 사흘이 되었는데,
v.WLB: 그러나 우리는 이스라엘을 구속해 주실 분이 바로 그분이라고 바라고 있었습니다. 그뿐 아니라 그런 일이 있은 지 벌써 3일째 됐는데

v.CNV: 而且我们中间有几个妇女使我们惊奇，她们清早到坟墓那里去，
v.ESV: Moreover, some women of our company amazed us. They were at the tomb early in the morning,
v.GAE: 또한 우리 중에 어떤 여자들이 우리로 놀라게 하였으니 이는 그들이 새벽에 무덤에 갔다가
v.KJV: Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
v.NASB: 'But also some women among us amazed us. When they were at the tomb early in the morning,
v.NIV: In addition, some of our women amazed us. They went to the tomb early this morning
v.NRSV: Moreover, some women of our group astounded us. They were at the tomb early this morning,
v.SAENEW: 우리 가운데서 몇몇 여자가 우리를 놀라게 하였습니다. 그들은 새벽에 무덤에 갔다가,
v.WLB: 우리 중 몇몇 여인들이 우리를 놀라게 했습니다. 그들이 아침 일찍 무덤에 갔다가

v.CNV: 却找不到他的身体。她们回来说看见天使显现，天使说他活了。
v.ESV: and when they did not find his body, they came back saying that they had even seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.
v.GAE: 그의 시체는 보지 못하고 와서 그가 살아나셨다 하는 천사들의 나타남을 보았다 함이라
v.KJV: And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they had also seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
v.NASB: and did not find His body, they came, saying that they had also seen a vision of angels who said that He was alive.
v.NIV: but didn’t find his body. They came and told us that they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.
v.NRSV: and when they did not find his body there, they came back and told us that they had indeed seen a vision of angels who said that he was alive.
v.SAENEW: 그의 시신을 찾지 못하고 돌아와서 하는 말이, 천사들의 환상을 보았다는 것입니다. 천사들이 예수가 살아 계신다고 말했다는 것입니다.
v.WLB: 그분의 시신을 찾지 못하고 돌아와서 천사들의 환상을 보았다고 했습니다. 그리고 그 천사들이 예수께서 살아 계신다고 말했다는 것입니다.

v.CNV: 又有我们中间的几个人到坟墓那里去，所看见的正如妇女们所说的，只是没有看见他。”
v.ESV: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but him they did not see.”
v.GAE: 또 우리와 함께 한 자 중에 두어 사람이 무덤에 가 과연 여자들이 말한 바와 같음을 보았으나 예수는 보지 못하였느니라 하거늘
v.KJV: And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found it even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
v.NASB: 'Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just exactly as the women also had said; but Him they did not see.'
v.NIV: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but they did not see Jesus.”
v.NRSV: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said; but they did not see him.”
v.SAENEW: 그래서 우리와 함께 있던 몇 사람이 무덤으로 가서 보니, 그 여자들이 말한 대로였고, 그분은 보지 못하였습니다."
v.WLB: 그래서 우리 동료 몇 사람이 무덤으로 가 보았더니 그 여인들이 말한 대로 그분을 볼 수 없었다는 것입니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“无知的人哪，先知所说的一切话，你们心里信得太迟钝了！
v.ESV: And he said to them, “O foolish ones, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
v.GAE: 이르시되 미련하고 선지자들이 말한 모든 것을 마음에 더디 믿는 자들이여
v.KJV: Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'O foolish men and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!
v.NIV: He said to them, “How foolish you are, and how slow to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
v.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Oh, how foolish you are, and how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have declared!
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "어리석은 사람들입니다. 예언자들이 말한 모든 것을 믿는 마음이 그렇게도 무디니 말입니다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “어리석고 예언자들이 말한 모든 것을 마음에 더디게 믿는 사람들이여!

v.CNV: 基督这样受害，然后进入他的荣耀，不是应当的吗？”
v.ESV: Was it not necessary that the Christ should suffer these things and enter into his glory?”
v.GAE: 그리스도가 이런 고난을 받고 자기의 영광에 들어가야 할 것이 아니냐 하시고
v.KJV: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
v.NASB: 'Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and to enter into His glory?'
v.NIV: Did not the Messiah have to suffer these things and then enter his glory?”
v.NRSV: Was it not necessary that the Messiah should suffer these things and then enter into his glory?”
v.SAENEW: 그리스도가 마땅히 이런 고난을 겪고서, 자기 영광에 들어가야 하지 않겠습니까?"
v.WLB: 그리스도께서 마땅히 이런 고난을 겪고서 자기 영광에 들어가야 할 것이 아니냐?”

v.CNV: 于是他从摩西和众先知起，把所有关于自己的经文，都给他们解释明白了。
v.ESV: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he interpreted to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.
v.GAE: 이에 모세와 모든 선지자의 글로 시작하여 모든 성경에 쓴 바 자기에 관한 것을 자세히 설명하시니라
v.KJV: And beginning at Moses and all the prophets, he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.
v.NASB: Then beginning with Moses and with all the prophets, He explained to them the things concerning Himself in all the Scriptures.
v.NIV: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he explained to them what was said in all the Scriptures concerning himself.
v.NRSV: Then beginning with Moses and all the prophets, he interpreted to them the things about himself in all the scriptures.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 예수께서는 모세와 모든 예언자에서부터 시작하여 성경 전체에서 자기에 관하여 써 놓은 일을 그들에게 설명하여 주셨다.
v.WLB: 그리고 예수께서는 모세와 모든 예언자들로부터 시작해 성경 전체에서 자기에 관해 언급된 것을 그들에게 자세히 설명해 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他们走近要去的村子，他好像还要往前行，
v.ESV: So they drew near to the village to which they were going. He acted as if he were going farther,
v.GAE: 그들이 가는 마을에 가까이 가매 예수는 더 가려 하는 것 같이 하시니
v.KJV: And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
v.NASB: And they approached the village where they were going, and He acted as though He were going farther.
v.NIV: As they approached the village to which they were going, Jesus continued on as if he were going farther.
v.NRSV: As they came near the village to which they were going, he walked ahead as if he were going on.
v.SAENEW: 그 두 길손은 자기들이 가려고 하는 마을에 가까이 이르렀다. 그런데 예수께서는 더 멀리 가는 척하셨다.
v.WLB: 그들이 가려던 엠마오 마을에 다다르자 예수께서는 더 가시려고 했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们强留他说：“天晚了，太阳下山了，请与我们同住吧！”他就进去与他们同住。
v.ESV: but they urged him strongly, saying, “Stay with us, for it is toward evening and the day is now far spent.” So he went in to stay with them.
v.GAE: 그들이 강권하여 이르되 우리와 함께 유하사이다 때가 저물어가고 날이 이미 기울었나이다 하니 이에 그들과 함께 유하러 들어가시니라
v.KJV: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
v.NASB: But they urged Him, saying, 'Stay with us, for it is getting toward evening, and the day is now nearly over.' So He went in to stay with them.
v.NIV: But they urged him strongly, “Stay with us, for it is nearly evening; the day is almost over.” So he went in to stay with them.
v.NRSV: But they urged him strongly, saying, “Stay with us, because it is almost evening and the day is now nearly over.” So he went in to stay with them.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 그들은 예수를 만류하여 말하였다. "저녁때가 되고, 날이 이미 저물었으니, 우리 집에 묵으십시오." 예수께서 그들의 집에 묵으려고 들어가셨다.
v.WLB: 그러자 그들이 예수를 한사코 말렸습니다. “저녁이 다 됐으니 여기서 우리와 함께 계시지요. 날이 다 저물었습니다.” 그래서 예수께서 그들과 함께 묵으려고 집에 들어가셨습니다.

v.CNV: 到了吃饭的时候，他拿起饼来，感谢了，擘开递给他们，
v.ESV: When he was at table with them, he took the bread and blessed and broke it and gave it to them.
v.GAE: 그들과 함께 음식 잡수실 때에 떡을 가지사 축사하시고 떼어 그들에게 주시니
v.KJV: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.
v.NASB: When He had reclined at the table with them, He took the bread and blessed it, and breaking it, He began giving it to them.
v.NIV: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, gave thanks, broke it and began to give it to them.
v.NRSV: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 그들과 함께 음식을 잡수시려고 앉으셨을 때에, 예수께서 빵을 들어서 축복하시고, 떼어서 그들에게 주셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들과 함께 상에 기대어 앉아 빵을 들고 감사기도를 드린 후 떼어 그들에게 나눠 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他们的眼睛开了，才认出是耶稣来；他却从他们面前不见了。
v.ESV: And their eyes were opened, and they recognized him. And he vanished from their sight.
v.GAE: 그들의 눈이 밝아져 그인 줄 알아 보더니 예수는 그들에게 보이지 아니하시는지라
v.KJV: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
v.NASB: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized Him; and He vanished from their sight.
v.NIV: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized him, and he disappeared from their sight.
v.NRSV: Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him; and he vanished from their sight.
v.SAENEW: 그제서야 그들의 눈이 열려서, 예수를 알아보았다. 그러나 한순간에 예수께서는 그들에게서 사라지셨다.
v.WLB: 그제야 그들의 눈이 열려 예수를 알아보았습니다. 그러나 곧 예수께서 그들의 눈앞에서 사라지셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他们彼此说：“在路上他对我们说话，给我们解释圣经的时候，我们的心不是火热的吗？”
v.ESV: They said to each other, “Did not our hearts burn within us while he talked to us on the road, while he opened to us the Scriptures?”
v.GAE: 그들이 서로 말하되 길에서 우리에게 말씀하시고 우리에게 성경을 풀어 주실 때에 우리 속에서 마음이 뜨겁지 아니하더냐 하고
v.KJV: And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
v.NASB: They said to one another, 'Were not our hearts burning within us while He was speaking to us on the road, while He was explaining the Scriptures to us?'
v.NIV: They asked each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?”
v.NRSV: They said to each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us while he was talking to us on the road, while he was opening the scriptures to us?”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 서로 말하였다. "길에서 그분이 우리에게 말씀하시고, 성경을 풀이하여 주실 때에, 우리의 마음이 [우리 속에서] 뜨거워지지 않았습니까?"
v.WLB: 그들이 서로 물었습니다. “길에서 그분이 우리에게 말씀하시고 성경을 풀어 주실 때 우리 마음이 뜨거워지지 않았느냐?”

v.CNV: 他们就立时起来回耶路撒冷去。在那里遇见十一个使徒和跟他们聚在一起的人，
v.ESV: And they rose that same hour and returned to Jerusalem. And they found the eleven and those who were with them gathered together,
v.GAE: 곧 그 때로 일어나 예루살렘에 돌아가 보니 열한 제자 및 그들과 함께 한 자들이 모여 있어
v.KJV: And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
v.NASB: And they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem, and found gathered together the eleven and those who were with them,
v.NIV: They got up and returned at once to Jerusalem. There they found the Eleven and those with them, assembled together
v.NRSV: That same hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem; and they found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 곧바로 일어나서, 예루살렘에 돌아와서 보니, 열한 제자와 또 그들과 함께 있던 사람들이 모여 있었고,
v.WLB: 그들이 즉시 일어나 예루살렘으로 돌아갔습니다. 가서 보니 거기에는 열한 제자가 다른 사람들과 함께 모여 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 说：“主果然复活了，已经向西门显现了。”
v.ESV: saying, “The Lord has risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”
v.GAE: 말하기를 주께서 과연 살아나시고 시몬에게 보이셨다 하는지라
v.KJV: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
v.NASB: saying, 'The Lord has really risen and has appeared to Simon.'
v.NIV: and saying, “It is true! The Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon.”
v.NRSV: They were saying, “The Lord has risen indeed, and he has appeared to Simon!”
v.SAENEW: 모두들 "주님께서 확실히 살아나시고, 시몬에게 나타나셨다" 하고 말하고 있었다.
v.WLB: 이들이 말했습니다. “주께서 참으로 살아나셨고 시몬에게 나타나셨다!”

v.CNV: 两个人就把在路上的事，和擘饼的时候他们怎样认出耶稣来的事，都述说了一遍。
v.ESV: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how he was known to them in the breaking of the bread.
v.GAE: 두 사람도 길에서 된 일과 예수께서 떡을 떼심으로 자기들에게 알려지신 것을 말하더라
v.KJV: And they told what things were done in the way, and how he was known of them in breaking of bread.
v.NASB: They began to relate their experiences on the road and how He was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.
v.NIV: Then the two told what had happened on the way, and how Jesus was recognized by them when he broke the bread.
v.NRSV: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how he had been made known to them in the breaking of the bread.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그 두 사람도 길에서 겪은 일과 빵을 떼실 때에 비로소 그를 알아보게 된 일을 이야기하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 그 두 사람도 길에서 있었던 일과 예수께서 빵을 떼어 주실 때 그들이 그분을 알아본 일을 이야기해 주었습니다.

v.CNV: 正说这话的时候，耶稣亲自站在他们当中，说：“愿你们平安。”
v.ESV: As they were talking about these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, “Peace to you!”
v.GAE: 이 말을 할 때에 예수께서 친히 그들 가운데 서서 이르시되 너희에게 평강이 있을지어다 하시니
v.KJV: And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
v.NASB: While they were telling these things, He Himself stood in their midst and said to them, 'Peace be to you.'
v.NIV: While they were still talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
v.NRSV: While they were talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 이런 이야기를 하고 있을 때에, 예수께서 몸소 그들 가운데 들어서서 말씀하셨다. "너희에게 평화가 있어라."
v.WLB: 그들이 아직 이런 이야기를 하고 있을 때 예수께서 바로 그들 사이에 나타나셔서 말씀하셨습니다. “너희에게 평화가 있으라.”

v.CNV: 他们非常惊怕，以为看见了灵。
v.ESV: But they were startled and frightened and thought they saw a spirit.
v.GAE: 그들이 놀라고 무서워하여 그 보는 것을 영으로 생각하는지라
v.KJV: But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
v.NASB: But they were startled and frightened and thought that they were seeing a spirit.
v.NIV: They were startled and frightened, thinking they saw a ghost.
v.NRSV: They were startled and terrified, and thought that they were seeing a ghost.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 놀라고, 무서움에 사로잡혀서, 유령을 보고 있는 줄로 생각하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 유령을 본 줄 알고 놀라며 무서워했습니다.

v.CNV: 他说：“你们为什么惊慌，为什么心里疑惑呢？
v.ESV: And he said to them, “Why are you troubled, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 어찌하여 두려워하며 어찌하여 마음에 의심이 일어나느냐
v.KJV: And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'Why are you troubled, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
v.NIV: He said to them, “Why are you troubled, and why do doubts rise in your minds?
v.NRSV: He said to them, “Why are you frightened, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "어찌하여 너희는 당황하느냐? 어찌하여 마음에 의심을 품느냐?
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “어째서 두려워하며 마음에 의심이 일어나느냐?

v.CNV: 你们看我的手、我的脚，就知道我是谁。摸我看看，灵没有骨，没有肉；你们看，我是有的。”
v.ESV: See my hands and my feet, that it is I myself. Touch me, and see. For a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have.”
v.GAE: 내 손과 발을 보고 나인 줄 알라 또 나를 만져 보라 영은 살과 뼈가 없으되 너희 보는 바와 같이 나는 있느니라
v.KJV: Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.
v.NASB: 'See My hands and My feet, that it is I Myself; touch Me and see, for a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have.'
v.NIV: Look at my hands and my feet. It is I myself! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have.”
v.NRSV: Look at my hands and my feet; see that it is I myself. Touch me and see; for a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have.”
v.SAENEW: 내 손과 내 발을 보아라. 바로 나다. 나를 만져 보아라. 유령은 살과 뼈가 없지만, 너희가 보다시피, 나는 살과 뼈가 있다."
v.WLB: 내 손과 내 발을 보라. 바로 나다! 나를 만져 보고 쳐다보라. 유령은 살과 뼈가 없다. 그러나 너희가 보다시피 나는 있지 않느냐?”

v.CNV: 说了这话，就把手和脚给他们看。
v.ESV: And when he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
v.GAE: 이 말씀을 하시고 손과 발을 보이시나
v.KJV: And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them his hands and his feet.
v.NASB: And when He had said this, He showed them His hands and His feet.
v.NIV: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
v.NRSV: And when he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 말씀하시고, 그는 손과 발을 그들에게 보이셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 이렇게 말씀하시고 그 손과 발을 보여 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他们欢喜到不敢相信，并且很惊奇。耶稣说：“你们这里有什么吃的没有？”
v.ESV: And while they still disbelieved for joy and were marveling, he said to them, “Have you anything here to eat?”
v.GAE: 그들이 너무 기쁘므로 아직도 믿지 못하고 놀랍게 여길 때에 이르시되 여기 무슨 먹을 것이 있느냐 하시니
v.KJV: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
v.NASB: While they still could not believe it because of their joy and amazement, He said to them, 'Have you anything here to eat?'
v.NIV: And while they still did not believe it because of joy and amazement, he asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
v.NRSV: While in their joy they were disbelieving and still wondering, he said to them, “Have you anything here to eat?”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 너무 기뻐서, 아직도 믿지 못하고 놀라워하고 있는데, 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "여기에 먹을 것이 좀 있느냐?"
v.WLB: 그들은 너무 기쁘고 놀라워 오히려 믿기지 않았습니다. 그때 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨습니다. “여기에 먹을 것이 좀 있느냐?”

v.CNV: 他们就给了他一片烧鱼。
v.ESV: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
v.GAE: 이에 구운 생선 한 토막을 드리니
v.KJV: And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
v.NASB: They gave Him a piece of a broiled fish;
v.NIV: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
v.NRSV: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그들이 예수께 구운 물고기 한 토막을 드렸다.
v.WLB: 그들은 구운 생선 한 토막을 갖다 드렸습니다.

v.CNV: 他接过来，在他们面前吃了。
v.ESV: and he took it and ate before them.
v.GAE: 받으사 그 앞에서 잡수시더라
v.KJV: And he took it, and did eat before them.
v.NASB: and He took it and ate it before them.
v.NIV: and he took it and ate it in their presence.
v.NRSV: and he took it and ate in their presence.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 받아서, 그들 앞에서 잡수셨다.
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서는 그들 앞에서 생선을 가져다가 잡수셨습니다.

v.CNV: 主对他们说：“这就是我从前与你们同在的时候，对你们说过的话：摩西的律法、先知书和诗篇上所记关于我的一切事，都必定应验。”
v.ESV: Then he said to them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you, that everything written about me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled.”
v.GAE: 또 이르시되 내가 너희와 함께 있을 때에 너희에게 말한 바 곧 모세의 율법과 선지자의 글과 시편에 나를 가리켜 기록된 모든 것이 이루어져야 하리라 한 말이 이것이라 하시고
v.KJV: And he said unto them, These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.
v.NASB: Now He said to them, 'These are My words which I spoke to you while I was still with you, that all things which are written about Me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled.'
v.NIV: He said to them, “This is what I told you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms.”
v.NRSV: Then he said to them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you — that everything written about me in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms must be fulfilled.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "내가 전에 너희와 함께 있을 때에 너희에게 말하기를, 모세의 율법과 예언서와 시편에 나를 두고 기록한 모든 일이 반드시 이루어져야 한다고 하였다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “내가 전에 너희와 함께 있을 때 모세의 율법과 예언서와 시편에서 나에 대해 기록된 모든 일이 마땅히 이루어져야 한다고 너희에게 말한 것이 바로 이것이다.”

v.CNV: 于是他开他们的心窍，使他们明白圣经；
v.ESV: Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures,
v.GAE: 이에 그들의 마음을 열어 성경을 깨닫게 하시고
v.KJV: Then opened he their understanding, that they might understand the scriptures,
v.NASB: Then He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures,
v.NIV: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures.
v.NRSV: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures,
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 예수께서는 성경을 깨닫게 하시려고, 그들의 마음을 열어 주시고,
v.WLB: 그리고 예수께서 그들의 마음을 열어 성경을 깨닫게 해 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 又说：“经上这样记着：基督必须受害，第三天从死人中复活。
v.ESV: and said to them, “Thus it is written, that the Christ should suffer and on the third day rise from the dead,
v.GAE: 또 이르시되 이같이 그리스도가 고난을 받고 제삼일에 죽은 자 가운데서 살아날 것과
v.KJV: And said unto them, Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day:
v.NASB: and He said to them, 'Thus it is written, that the Christ would suffer and rise again from the dead the third day,
v.NIV: He told them, “This is what is written: The Messiah will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
v.NRSV: and he said to them, “Thus it is written, that the Messiah is to suffer and to rise from the dead on the third day,
v.SAENEW: 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "이렇게 기록되어 있다. 곧 '그리스도는 고난을 겪으시고, 사흘째 되는 날에 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살아나실 것이며,
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “이렇게 기록돼 있다. 그리스도께서 고난을 겪고 3일째 되는 날 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살아날 것이며

v.CNV: 人要奉他的名，传讲悔改与赦罪的道，从耶路撒冷起，直传到万国。
v.ESV: and that repentance for the forgiveness of sins should be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
v.GAE: 또 그의 이름으로 죄 사함을 받게 하는 회개가 예루살렘에서 시작하여 모든 족속에게 전파될 것이 기록되었으니
v.KJV: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
v.NASB: and that repentance for forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in His name to all the nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
v.NIV: and repentance for the forgiveness of sins will be preached in his name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
v.NRSV: and that repentance and forgiveness of sins is to be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
v.SAENEW: 그의 이름으로 죄사함을 받게 하는 회개가 모든 민족에게 전파될 것이다' 하였다. 예루살렘에서부터 시작하여
v.WLB: 또 예루살렘으로부터 시작해 모든 민족에게 그의 이름으로 죄 용서를 받게 하는 회개가 전파될 것이다.

v.CNV: 你们就是这些事的见证。
v.ESV: You are witnesses of these things.
v.GAE: 너희는 이 모든 일의 증인이라
v.KJV: And ye are witnesses of these things.
v.NASB: 'You are witnesses of these things.
v.NIV: You are witnesses of these things.
v.NRSV: You are witnesses of these things.
v.SAENEW: 너희는 이 일의 증인이다.
v.WLB: 너희는 이 일들의 증인이다.

v.CNV: 我要使我父的应许临到你们身上，你们当在城里等候，直到得着从上面来的能力。”
v.ESV: And behold, I am sending the promise of my Father upon you. But stay in the city until you are clothed with power from on high.”
v.GAE: 볼지어다 내가 내 아버지께서 약속하신 것을 너희에게 보내리니 너희는 위로부터 능력으로 입혀질 때까지 이 성에 머물라 하시니라
v.KJV: And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.
v.NASB: 'And behold, I am sending forth the promise of My Father upon you; but you are to stay in the city until you are clothed with power from on high.'
v.NIV: I am going to send you what my Father has promised; but stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.”
v.NRSV: And see, I am sending upon you what my Father promised; so stay here in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.”
v.SAENEW: [보아라,] 나는 내 아버지께서 약속하신 것을 너희에게 보낸다. 그러므로 너희는 위로부터 오는 능력을 입을 때까지, 이 성에 머물러 있어라."
v.WLB: 보라. 내가 내 아버지께서 약속하신 것을 너희에게 보낸다. 그러므로 너희는 위로부터 내려오는 능력을 입을 때까지 예루살렘에 머물러 있으라.”

v.CNV: 主带他们出去，到了伯大尼附近，举手给他们祝福。
v.ESV: And he led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands he blessed them.
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들을 데리고 베다니 앞까지 나가사 손을 들어 그들에게 축복하시더니
v.KJV: And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
v.NASB: And He led them out as far as Bethany, and He lifted up His hands and blessed them.
v.NIV: When he had led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, he lifted up his hands and blessed them.
v.NRSV: Then he led them out as far as Bethany, and, lifting up his hands, he blessed them.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 예수께서는 그들을 [밖으로] 베다니까지 데리고 가서, 손을 들어 그들을 축복하셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 제자들을 이끌고 베다니 앞에까지 가시더니 거기서 두 손을 들고 그들에게 복을 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 正在祝福的时候，他就离开他们，被接到天上去了。
v.ESV: While he blessed them, he parted from them and was carried up into heaven.
v.GAE: 축복하실 때에 그들을 떠나 [하늘로 올려지시니]
v.KJV: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and carried up into heaven.
v.NASB: While He was blessing them, He parted from them and was carried up into heaven.
v.NIV: While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken up into heaven.
v.NRSV: While he was blessing them, he withdrew from them and was carried up into heaven.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 그들을 축복하시는 가운데, 그들에게서 떠나 하늘로 올라가셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 제자들에게 복을 주시는 중에 그들을 떠나 [하늘로 들려 올라가]셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他们就敬拜他，欢欢喜喜地回到耶路撒冷，
v.ESV: And they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
v.GAE: 그들이 [그에게 경배하고] 큰 기쁨으로 예루살렘에 돌아가
v.KJV: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
v.NASB: And they, after worshiping Him, returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
v.NIV: Then they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
v.NRSV: And they worshiped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy;
v.SAENEW: 그들은 예수께 경배하고, 크게 기뻐하면서, 예루살렘으로 돌아가서,
v.WLB: 그러자 그들은 [예수께 경배하며] 기쁨에 넘쳐 예루살렘으로 돌아가

v.CNV: 常常在殿里称颂　神。
v.ESV: and were continually in the temple blessing God.
v.GAE: 늘 성전에서 하나님을 찬송하니라
v.KJV: And were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
v.NASB: and were continually in the temple praising God.
v.NIV: And they stayed continually at the temple, praising God.
v.NRSV: and they were continually in the temple blessing God.
v.SAENEW: 하나님을 찬양하면서 날마다 성전에서 지냈다.
v.WLB: 하나님을 찬양하면서 계속 성전에 있었습니다.
